title: 'podman inspect --format json --type container' returns State:Pid as float64
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

When trying to parse the `Pid` field out of the JSON returned by `podman inspect --type container` in Go with `encoding/json`, the PID value is a `float64`.

Reproducer:
```
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"encoding/json"
	"errors"
	"fmt"
	"os"
	"os/exec"
)

func main() {
	if len(os.Args) != 2 {
		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Error: wrong arguments\n")
		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Expected: %s CONTAINER\n", os.Args[0])
		os.Exit(1)
	}

	container := os.Args[1]
	args := []string{"inspect", "--format", "json", "--type", "container", container}

	cmd := exec.Command("podman", args...)

	var stdout bytes.Buffer
	cmd.Stdout = &stdout

	if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil {
		if errors.Is(err, exec.ErrNotFound) {
			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Error: podman(1) not found\n")
			os.Exit(1)
		}

		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Error: failed to run 'podman inspect': %v\n", err)
		os.Exit(1)
	}

	output := stdout.Bytes()

	var info []map[string]interface{}
	if err := json.Unmarshal(output, &info); err != nil {
		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Error: failed to parse JSON: %v\n", err)
		os.Exit(1)
	}

	state := info[0]["State"].(map[string]interface{})
	pid := state["Pid"]
	fmt.Printf("State:Pid is %T\n", pid)
}
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

The `Pid` value should be an integer type.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.9.1
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13.10
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-1.9.1-1.fc31.x86_64
```

comments.body: Thats how json works. It only knows numbers, so when you parse something using the standard json decoder into an `interface{}` it will be converted to a `float64` because there is no more type information in the json.

> Thats how json works. It only knows numbers, so when you parse something using the standard json decoder into an `interface{}` it will be converted to a `float64` because there is no more type information in the json.

Interesting. I did not know that. Then I guess this can be closed because this is not a bug.
